name: Blue House [via DOTL]
subject: Thousands of votes left to count
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Democracy on the Line PAC
body:
First few lines

|
---
Peter, we need your help.

As of now, there’s still thousands of votes to be counted in some of the most competitive House districts across the country.

We must ensure each and every one of these ballots are counted so we can elect representatives that believe in protecting reproductive freedoms, democracy, and Medicare.

That’s why we are contacting voters to help make sure that rejected ballots are fixed and submitted and sending our staff to these six battleground districts to monitor vote counting.

**But, Peter, this work is expensive and we’re running out of time and money.** [**Will you chip in a split donation between our team and Democracy on the Line to help our team on the ground fight until every last vote is counted and support these five battleground districts? We must ensure each and every one of these ballots are counted so we can elect representatives that believe in protecting reproductive freedoms, democracy, and Medicare. That’s why we are contacting voters to help make sure that rejected ballots are fixed and submitted and sending our staff to these six battleground districts to monitor vote counting. name: Hillsdale College via Red Conservatives
subject: Our nation’s history is unifying
body:
I’m reaching out to share something important with you...something that could expand your horizons. | _Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this[link](http://links.redconservatives.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=66ce1250519441959ebe70cf27686494&_s=6129c70d1ae84c52bebc7d18432467a5&_t=fAs-BAAtGmEtcSm6_HdxGtd3Dft4dSnY-llrjVLyBTVKlDm7Jp-lGQWuYyt1ro0gASYqY1xvKQzoE9vg0Cwj1Kt_fFo6vV0HbEKVvaLSXMC1Vzza2lkVIUrJWnhtMUUR)_
---
[](http://links.redconservatives.com/u/click?_t=66ce1250519441959ebe70cf27686494&_m=6129c70d1ae84c52bebc7d18432467a5&_e=k83S1ofeF4eACHEhfwl6ofeSqD4vhxP1PGRxTZxiEPCSMtCdlNawRMFs7tzn6mQmi6WLiHwRXIVm21T04ZPuME71I7Cj6DLx3N6a0yl_L5Xt1mFKrAxd4emZFhg3M-KBWOBAFKaHLAtWWGtsOXW7K735ER11OYmc_WFh9mhTDmIeMa1qId1s6G7kiXShTqWfUn37e5smYvU0m3_30M-RF6XStpRC3qaRGTaniSGZiJjpoaxbrF9zh-apjlLPRc4Mi2HB2hfB8qK9HQJNo2WvlaFxDrBr60NbtRU1zCqCcgwAwcl9qLEXXMrf4fFpYsqxKtUBik9UrYplI0tq6cE8iX2sytalAvt-jIU5i3hE8JJ5FYYX0hHTf7DVlVeiZQ5zDuyg8qZyI4Rr9FguNkx_nEhYWBK_IveiuQFg8_YrYzo%3D) Dear Fellow American, Thanks for opening my email. I hope you're having a great day so far. I'm reaching out to share something important with you...something that could expand your horizons. I'm talking about studying American history! As Hillsdale College professor Wilfred McClay explains in our online course ["The Great American Story: A Land of Hope,"](http://links.redconservatives.com/u/click?_t=66ce1250519441959ebe70cf27686494&_m=6129c70d1ae84c52bebc7d18432467a5&_e=k83S1ofeF4eACHEhfwl6ofeSqD4vhxP1PGRxTZxiEPCSMtCdlNawRMFs7tzn6mQmi6WLiHwRXIVm21T04ZPuME71I7Cj6DLx3N6a0yl_L5Xt1mFKrAxd4emZFhg3M-KBWOBAFKaHLAtWWGtsOXW7K735ER11OYmc_WFh9mhTDmIeMa1qId1s6G7kiXShTqWfUn37e5smYvU0m3_30M-RF6XStpRC3qaRGTaniSGZiJjpoaxbrF9zh-apjlLPRc4Mi2HB2hfB8qK9HQJNo2WvlUHf2yL8MAbC4PbsjIqtBSroTkRwh9gEGlFkGiPeb13q7Um48p1IUi9HK_cO11vNIpBOKaO0umW9oMvCurk2TcIV-AbTEAAw3Z2VYFYk7_7eoFh81iknNHClmHciGSnIFFP9J0SPSdhuC7-RCar-HM8%3D) studying history can broaden your perspective and help you understand different ways of living. Compare that to _The New York Times '_ "1619 Project," now a multi-part series streaming online. The "1619 Project" locates the founding of America in 1619, the year the first slave arrived on our shores, rather than in 1776, when the **Declaration of Independence declared that all men are created equal with unalienable God-given rights**. According to the "1619 Project," the key to understanding America is not freedom but slavery--and the project's main goal is to broadcast this false teaching into every home, including yours. We must counter this false and dishonest historical narrative with the truth. **That 's why we've turned our online course, ["The Great American Story: A Land of Hope,"](http://links.redconservatives.com/u/click?_t=66ce1250519441959ebe70cf27686494&_m=6129c70d1ae84c52bebc7d18432467a5&_e=k83S1ofeF4eACHEhfwl6ofeSqD4vhxP1PGRxTZxiEPCSMtCdlNawRMFs7tzn6mQmi6WLiHwRXIVm21T04ZPuME71I7Cj6DLx3N6a0yl_L5Xt1mFKrAxd4emZFhg3M-KBWOBAFKaHLAtWWGtsOXW7K735ER11OYmc_WFh9mhTDmIeMa1qId1s6G7kiXShTqWfUn37e5smYvU0m3_30M-RF6XStpRC3qaRGTaniSGZiJjpoaxbrF9zh-apjlLPRc4Mi2HB2hfB8qK9HQJNo2WvlUplm9gpTYVW7A2VHdOZj5-z8_JSWryXTHhzUa1tJVwIJRje-JbOMLxye-sAwnXS23E-rjo6kF7XFVbjzPIwTtrSA9wrvPj_E8OObUoXhyJf6LNdPND_IVlviwlHTI3JEhJcqCoUNp4PjnjsBMSKFs4%3D) into a DVD set, and I'd like you to have a copy.** **" The Great American Story: A Land of Hope"** presents a full, unbiased account of America's past. We produced this course to restore the kind of intelligent patriotism--especially among our young people--essential to restoring free government. You'll be excited to learn that **" The Great American Story: A Land of Hope"** is a powerful counterweight to _The New York Times '_ "1619 Project," which tells people, especially children, that America is essentially an unjust nation--the opposite of our nation's unifying principles. Today, you can claim a DVD copy of ["The Great American Story: A Land of Hope"](http://links.redconservatives.com/u/click?_t=66ce1250519441959ebe70cf27686494&_m=6129c70d1ae84c52bebc7d18432467a5&_e=k83S1ofeF4eACHEhfwl6ofeSqD4vhxP1PGRxTZxiEPCSMtCdlNawRMFs7tzn6mQmi6WLiHwRXIVm21T04ZPuME71I7Cj6DLx3N6a0yl_L5Xt1mFKrAxd4emZFhg3M-KBWOBAFKaHLAtWWGtsOXW7K735ER11OYmc_WFh9mhTDmIeMa1qId1s6G7kiXShTqWfUn37e5smYvU0m3_30M-RF6XStpRC3qaRGTaniSGZiJjpoaxbrF9zh-apjlLPRc4Mi2HB2hfB8qK9HQJNo2WvleIiETXB_dbFTTZt23van5UgibL3JGjM8OGlBDX5_irZ0nu9VTkCVLQRrZ1bqWuYWICJBg7SEueiLkDYrnaUCqNCGJ6evd2sQndgVDE2U219sK1duNiRtqS47L5oEtxw-Wulhngd9SE4-dlTfquBbho%3D) for your home library or give it as a gift. **Presenting real American history is the surest means of preserving liberty.** Quantities are limited, so reserve your copy today. [https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/support-great-american-story-dvd](http://links.redconservatives.com/u/click?_t=66ce1250519441959ebe70cf27686494&_m=6129c70d1ae84c52bebc7d18432467a5&_e=k83S1ofeF4eACHEhfwl6ofeSqD4vhxP1PGRxTZxiEPCSMtCdlNawRMFs7tzn6mQmi6WLiHwRXIVm21T04ZPuME71I7Cj6DLx3N6a0yl_L5Xt1mFKrAxd4emZFhg3M-KBWOBAFKaHLAtWWGtsOXW7K735ER11OYmc_WFh9mhTDmIeMa1qId1s6G7kiXShTqWfUn37e5smYvU0m3_30M-RF6XStpRC3qaRGTaniSGZiJjpoaxbrF9zh-apjlLPRc4Mi2HB2hfB8qK9HQJNo2WvlZKX7FTZPIGxI4KG3WkD6DUWrfKQy9AxbSCbQ3j8pJXKoXvVj693iwH6dvexkQ5z9Whi7gQwK81n5LGfCLqyx8zvocKlZV_6DsMpvlqdSjGNjVOD1cFuddT8R04Y-cveZ7R_3wq45Pbnsbes9bXvqcs%3D) Thank you for your partnership with Hillsdale College and for supporting its mission on behalf of liberty. Warm regards, Bill Gray, '01 Vice President Institutional Advancement Hillsdale College **P.S.** Despite what _The New York Times '_ "1619 Project" says, our nation's history is unifying. That's why we've produced a full, unbiased account of America's past with **" The Great American Story: A Land of Hope,"** now available on DVD. You can receive your copy with your generous tax-deductible gift of $100 or more using this secure link: [https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/support-great-american-story-dvd](http://links.redconservatives.com/u/click?_t=66ce1250519441959ebe70cf27686494&_m=6129c70d1ae84c52bebc7d18432467a5&_e=k83S1ofeF4eACHEhfwl6ofeSqD4vhxP1PGRxTZxiEPCSMtCdlNawRMFs7tzn6mQmi6WLiHwRXIVm21T04ZPuME71I7Cj6DLx3N6a0yl_L5Xt1mFKrAxd4emZFhg3M-KBWOBAFKaHLAtWWGtsOXW7K735ER11OYmc_WFh9mhTDmIeMa1qId1s6G7kiXShTqWfUn37e5smYvU0m3_30M-RF6XStpRC3qaRGTaniSGZiJjpoaxbrF9zh-apjlLPRc4Mi2HB2hfB8qK9HQJNo2WvlbdAxFSpp-583E6SMruk3Qndwj_8JAdrAfJCZlHeg2fovOzED9vDAxtioEnwiEatg8gBLlzoBgGIlMdt-1Ltem0waZZ5BYRow59XCKt3Aq-8m74NAHGo6ea_NkEeOY0NEY9hDbjDeSZqbbw4LEOIrRg%3D) Act today because quantities are limited! Thank you so much for helping us promote intelligent patriotism--especially among younger Americans. |
---

name: The New Pennsylvania Project
subject: Your Bi-Weekly NPP eBlast
body:
| | |
---
| | Hi Friend , Welcome to the latest New Pennsylvania Project newsletter! While the presidential, several statewide and congressional results were not exactly what we hoped for, we are proud of the work that we have accomplished together.

We are excited to see Speaker Joanna McClinton retain the gavel and State Senator-elect Patty Kim elevated in Dauphin County to name a few.

Our work is far from finished. In 2025, PA will elect judges and justices in partisan elections and participate in nonpartisan judicial retention elections, including three PA Supreme Court seats.We need to make sure that our communities do not lose hope, and turnout in 2025 for our local, municipal and judicial elections. An independent and fair judiciary is needed now more than ever before in the Commonwealth.

It’s critical that we remain vigilant and work together to protect the future of our democracy.#WhenWeVoteWeWin
---
|
---
---
# Native American Heritage Month
---
The Thanksgiving holidays might be at the top of mind during November for most people, but we want to take this time to amplify the voices and stories of the original inhabitants of the United States and their descendants.According to the U.S. Census and the PA Department of State, 52% of Indigenous people in Pennsylvania are not registered to vote or are at risk of being dropped from the voter rolls.
---
# Do you know who originally inhabited the land you now occupy?

---
---
The map above shows the original borders of the Indigenous people's land before colonization. The stars indicate the location our eight offices.
---
[ **LEARN MORE ABOUT THE LAND YOU OCCUPY**](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9YiUCH1GHoQaJPiTk-2FrDXtwhFYAJZ-2FhIsaL6Qivebjn9agDZx_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVAKmTIthZAqug-2BBe3TY9jweA5gYJTWBMeK2mbEu1GsUAWbMxqbbanoccXqIiueqpc6DIzHn4ElffI7A3Y14vKdCNksTNdqpcPsZ-2Fywu-2FMuXA4y3CGiU9X4tiAddJTMlizHBV3fCrFnIJPehcpV1HfrYvCgq4D1r1cXU-2FH39WWyfa5wztCzVL9MEYbf73cVD79djeA8jKkdCCT-2BYZ3uzuqxVvSyEoM8n9o8svZWrYtkrdwbOxxYMTqNt0728C-2F2GjmLODC2V0GFc7W35XgFNtXCVclKhxVvGqNYsL5HxzrLWz2AD5hkDObMwW81XYanSnmDQ-2BYS1D2qVfRRCDlE4B8ad)
---
---
[**According to IWPR**](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9YjPggog7lSP6xzkemSxR6PZpEvEh-2Fy7TyjVIGyZMaAaTXkRqw7WB82CKaG8vOipEaZDqGfGPrYzxdXyE101NQ1s-3Druat_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVAKmTIthZAqug-2BBe3TY9jweA5gYJTWBMeK2mbEu1GsUAWbMxqbbanoccXqIiueqpc6DIzHn4ElffI7A3Y14vKdCNksTNdqpcPsZ-2Fywu-2FMuXA4y3CGiU9X4tiAddJTMlizHBV3fCrFnIJPehcpV1HfrYvCgq4D1r1cXU-2FH39WWyfa5wztCzVL9MEYbf73cVD79cZ4E5mxoyWtfmXINNICOKBIhpQx58DUnSz0SWAKeD-2F-2FsRxUAPTHfhBpM58gXa593LJsFeyVlwfBsQnBeivOnAWz-2B2mN6-2BXZxd8abovmoBpbSD98HB-2FsOzd6cdfduriUPclg-2Ff3-2FyG4TuWs35OgeHsW) , Native American women are not expected to reach pay equity with white men until 2144.
---
**A short history on Native American rights:**
---

**[Voting inequalities for Native Americans still exist ](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9YtaMoaIjFg8G9pOXbTw0oMWAEIcf2ixmYeW3LOy-2FWfgkfTIKlk7gFMRccr5WeylxJGZtNJWX60aB1VL-2B5Zl8L7Sxuh6Z-2Bw59n-2F7xgdIFdWJoFDaoVW4p5YKdz4WfWGR5TiQsxA6us9V0Klp5sQKgyK4-3DzMDF_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVAKmTIthZAqug-2BBe3TY9jweA5gYJTWBMeK2mbEu1GsUAWbMxqbbanoccXqIiueqpc6DIzHn4ElffI7A3Y14vKdCNksTNdqpcPsZ-2Fywu-2FMuXA4y3CGiU9X4tiAddJTMlizHBV3fCrFnIJPehcpV1HfrYvCgq4D1r1cXU-2FH39WWyfa5wztCzVL9MEYbf73cVD79egXzEFKvVeiyNdOcaTziF5cL7Ka346A7yDI5x3Bnuo90EXgO4WkpKlhZ4J0n9nuZCek0AQHIBYtApayIW5sxUFfQcMrY-2BOz00KS0OQOdiZP-2Bi2nNgwrnucLf0quzl1takykn2ljyC-2BUTyATjViiUCj)**nearly a century after Congress granted them citizenship. clean_body: | | | | | Hi Friend , Welcome to the latest New Pennsylvania Project newsletter! While the presidential, several statewide and congressional results were not exactly what we hoped for, we are proud of the work that we have accomplished together. We are excited to see Speaker Joanna McClinton retain the gavel and State Senator-elect Patty Kim elevated in Dauphin County to name a few. Our work is far from finished. In 2025, PA will elect judges and justices in partisan elections and participate in nonpartisan judicial retention elections, including three PA Supreme Court seats.We need to make sure that our communities do not lose hope, and turnout in 2025 for our local, municipal and judicial elections. An independent and fair judiciary is needed now more than ever before in the Commonwealth. It’s critical that we remain vigilant and work together to protect the future of our democracy.#WhenWeVoteWeWin | Native American Heritage Month The Thanksgiving holidays might be at the top of mind during November for most people, but we want to take this time to amplify the voices and stories of the original inhabitants of the United States and their descendants.According to the U.S. Census and the PA Department of State, 52% of Indigenous people in Pennsylvania are not registered to vote or are at risk of being dropped from the voter rolls. Do you know who originally inhabited the land you now occupy? The map above shows the original borders of the Indigenous people's land before colonization. The stars indicate the location our eight offices. , Native American women are not expected to reach pay equity with white men until 2144. A short history on Native American rights: a century after Congress granted them citizenship. This is unacceptable, and a way to fix this is by voicing our vote. Participation in electoral politics can be conflicting for many people, but where history has failed, voting offers a path forward. | NPP ON THE GROUND | | We left it all on the ground During the two weeks of our GOTV program, our goal was to knock on 41,000 doors statewide. We exceeded our goal by 28%. Philly Collar Counties | | | | | Chester County | Montgomery County | | Northampton County | | Lebanon County | | Capital Region | Erie | | | SOCIAL OF THE WEEK | | | | | Let's not forget to celebrate the historic wins from this Executive of Prince George's County Angela Alsobrooks: Maryland’s first Black female U.S. Senator Delaware State Rep Lisa Blunt Rochester: Delaware’s first Black and first female U.S. Senator New Jersey State Rep Andy Kim: The country’s first Korean-American U.S. Senator Delaware State Senator Sarah McBride: The first openly transgender member in the U.S. Congress We're still making history. | | | NPP IN THE NEWS In an article titled the Philadelphia Inquirer, Avi Steinhardt wrote: "Kadida Kenner , CEO of the New Pennsylvania Project, said that her voting rights organization has worked “tirelessly” to combat GOP challenges." In an article titled The Keystone, Ashley Adams wrote: "Frustrated with the election results? You can still lend a hand in supporting the issues that matter most to you by volunteering at these organizations... The is a voting rights organization focused on voter registration, civic education, and mobilization." Election Law Blog also made a summary of top suits mentioning our organization. | | | #COURTSMATTER “The Republican petitioners claim that some counties — including Bucks County, Centre County and Philadelphia — chose to count undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots in the November 2024 general election. They argue this violates the state Supreme Court’s orders in two , which prohibited the counting of undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots in the November election.” “On Thursday, the RNC and McCormick a lawsuit in Centre County, the Centre County Board of Elections’ decision to count three mail-in ballots submitted with undated or wrongly dated outer envelopes in the general election. The plaintiffs argue that this violates the Pennsylvania Supreme Court’s decision in a similar recent lawsuit, which prohibited undated or wrongly dated ballots from being counted in the general election.” “On Thursday, a New York trial court judge struck down the state’s 2022 Voting Rights Act (NYVRA), which was created to make up for deficiencies in federal law following Congress’s 2021 failure to pass the John R. Lewis Voting Rights Act... Citing the 2023 U.S. Supreme Court ruling that determined race-based affirmative action in college admissions was unconstitutional, Vazquez-Doles wrote that the NYVRA similarly “provides enhanced rights” to protected classes, in violation of the Constitution. ” “Ohio Attorney General David Yost (R) the petition summary of the Ohio Voters Bill of Rights, a proposed pro-voting constitutional amendment that would greatly expand ballot access in the Buckeye State.” | NEWS YOU CAN USE “The Pennsylvania Department of State ordered a recount to be conducted in the state’s U.S. Senate race, since the margin is less than 0.5%, triggering an automatic recount. Under Pennsylvania law, if a race qualifies for an automatic recount — and the losing candidate doesn’t waive it — the state must order a recount by 5 p.m. on the second Thursday following Election Day.” “Prior to the election, a Democracy Docket investigation hundreds of election deniers on the ballot in seven key states — Arizona, Florida, Georgia, Michigan, North Carolina, Pennsylvania and Wisconsin. All of the candidates identified in the investigation were incumbents serving local office in positions like elections commissioners, municipal clerks, board of commissioners members and state lawmakers.” “This year’s general election saw dozens of candidates win seats on state high courts, with Democrats and Republicans securing respective majorities in Michigan and Ohio. One new justice even made history. Although most of the supreme court races weren’t partisan, the two political parties vied for the opportunity to control the court’s ideological balance, which can impact how high courts rule.” “Though a woman of color will not be heading to the White House this year, history was still made with the election of Angela Alsobrooks (D) in Maryland and Rep. Lisa Blunt Rochester (D) in Delaware – marking the first time ever that two Black women will serve together in the U.S. Senate.” “Latinos, young men, non-college-educated white people, suburban women. The exit polls and political analysis invariably focuses on the changing behavior of demographic groups. That ignores a big determinant of political behavior: where people get their news and information. It’s odd how little attention has been given to this, given that in the past decade we’ve had a revolution in how information flows.” “President-elect Donald Trump’s plan to carry out mass deportations of undocumented immigrants would lead to population loss, exacerbate workforce shortages and increase tax burdens for Pennsylvania residents and businesses, the Democratic state lawmakers heard this week.” | | ELECTION RESOURCES The Supreme Court won't block the counting of certain provisional ballots in battleground Pennsylvania. If you voted using a provisional ballot, you can find its status . | | | Important Links: Voter Hotlines: Voter Protection Hotline: 1-866-OUR-VOTEDOS PA Voter Hotline: 1-877-VOTESPA Spanish/English Hotline: 1-866-VE-Y-VOTAAsian Languages Hotline: 1-888-API-VOTE Arabic/English Hotline: 1-844-YALLA-US | | Our Contact Information New Pennsylvania Project P.O. Box 443 West Chester, PA 19381 4126996771 | | | | | ---|---|---

Peter, 99% of people who receive this email _won’t even finish reading this sentence._ We get it. $10, $20, or even $5 a donation might not seem like it can do a lot. But when thousands of people are donating all across the country, it can go a long way in defending our democracy. The first $41,300/$15,000 from a person/multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to the DNC. The next $3,300/$5,000 from a person/PAC will be allocated to Harris for President’s Recount Account. The next $510,000/$255,000 from a person/PAC will be split equally among the Democratic state parties from these states: AK, AL, AR, AZ, CA, CO, CT, DC, DE, FL, GA, HI, IA, ID, IL, IN, KS, KY, LA, MA, MD, ME, MI, MN, MO, MS, MT, NC, ND, NE, NH, NJ, NM, NV, NY, OH, OK, OR, PA, RI, SC, SD, TN, TX, UT, VA, VT, WA, WI, WV, and WY. Any additional funds will be allocated to the DNC, subject to applicable contribution limits.
A contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant by contacting **info@kamalaharris.com**.The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution.
clean_body: | | | Peter, 99% of people who receive this email won’t even finish reading this sentence. We get it. $10, $20, or even $5 a donation might not seem like it can do a lot. But when thousands of people are donating all across the country, it can go a long way in defending our democracy. Peter, we have staff on the ground continuing to support the races that need our help. We're ready to defend the races that are within the margin of recounts or certain legal challenges and that is going to take some time. But all of this takes resources, Peter, so we're relying on you today. If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | Thanks for your support, Team Harris-Walz | | | |

I’ve said it before: **The outcome of an election can easily become a Rorschach test on which we project our own ideological priors.** In order to avoid substituting narrative for truth, _we must go where the evidence takes us._

body:
Today’s National Philanthropy Day and we want to celebrate you. | | | [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/97164933/512517328/-1698714710?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjNhMDVmNTFiLWI2YTMtZWYxMS04OGQwLTYwNDViZGQ2MmRiNiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=6enCTZyMA5i44Xelf5wm1uLXecgdDvABi_OeG2QLmMo=&emci=c919935c-9ea3-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&emdi=3a05f51b-b6a3-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&ceid=2107230) Hi Peter, Today is National Philanthropy Day, and we wanted to take a moment to say thank you to all of our incredible members. To show our appreciation, we’re sending you these exclusive OLCV member backgrounds of the Owyhee Canyonlands. [Click here to download a wallpaper for your phone](https://click.everyaction.com/k/97164934/512517345/-875515397?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjNhMDVmNTFiLWI2YTMtZWYxMS04OGQwLTYwNDViZGQ2MmRiNiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=6enCTZyMA5i44Xelf5wm1uLXecgdDvABi_OeG2QLmMo=&emci=c919935c-9ea3-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&emdi=3a05f51b-b6a3-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&ceid=2107230), and [click here to download a wallpaper for your computer](https://click.everyaction.com/k/97164936/512517358/-1618559620?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjNhMDVmNTFiLWI2YTMtZWYxMS04OGQwLTYwNDViZGQ2MmRiNiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=6enCTZyMA5i44Xelf5wm1uLXecgdDvABi_OeG2QLmMo=&emci=c919935c-9ea3-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&emdi=3a05f51b-b6a3-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&ceid=2107230). We have lots of work ahead of us in 2025, but I know that we can do it together. To further our cause, [make a contribution today by clicking here](https://click.everyaction.com/k/97164938/512517373/-1698714710?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjNhMDVmNTFiLWI2YTMtZWYxMS04OGQwLTYwNDViZGQ2MmRiNiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=6enCTZyMA5i44Xelf5wm1uLXecgdDvABi_OeG2QLmMo=&emci=c919935c-9ea3-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&emdi=3a05f51b-b6a3-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&ceid=2107230) or [sign up to volunteer here](https://click.everyaction.com/k/97164939/512517385/-600427321?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjNhMDVmNTFiLWI2YTMtZWYxMS04OGQwLTYwNDViZGQ2MmRiNiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=6enCTZyMA5i44Xelf5wm1uLXecgdDvABi_OeG2QLmMo=&emci=c919935c-9ea3-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&emdi=3a05f51b-b6a3-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&ceid=2107230) if you haven’t already. [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/97164940/512517392/-1698714710?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjNhMDVmNTFiLWI2YTMtZWYxMS04OGQwLTYwNDViZGQ2MmRiNiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=6enCTZyMA5i44Xelf5wm1uLXecgdDvABi_OeG2QLmMo=&emci=c919935c-9ea3-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&emdi=3a05f51b-b6a3-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&ceid=2107230) Thank you for all that you do. name: Patti Reilly
subject: Free my police officer son
body:
Never in my wildest imagination could I have predicted that my son would be wrongfully convicted of murder – murder of a criminal my son didn’t even touch.
Dear Peter,

My son, Washington Metro Police Officer Terence Sutton, was just sentenced to 5 1⁄2 years in prison for doing the job he was trained to do.

And though you and I have never met, I’m praying you’ll help me save him.

Here are some photos of Terence as a little boy.

[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=19870903dcac47978f096cb621b219fc&_e=VfO086_jlF6Od5lAsO5tjdpDpA6ZKg0GhnM1qVP-NtElyut7eJjinnAsHj3_CI3NFsGXzFWbL-Ebyw9_VqSfSiG6sQJtnCD3oc-otN2DR4y5nf5e811A5IbglH9ERKQy9ZTkubcGpOFKGn4GQZefQEpJw-RVLU_s280heWhkxiXSp2k1w3MxSpAOPGrdZzJ4uSFB9jRhIMAEAriPuaRyYHreLqhCcwMIHG4O4XhSiTn3V8ZuEQ74pt6MxN1RiF3gBGL_e7E5ftUeFTqbdKyugLUKr6_cHc7bk5iY8dPLSlGeUW3t5WAKhWQYihsxjpNPhrBWgxfG2-cpqv1CHH9-cmqfm49712PbgmJjUMeSyNJ5xlIDL75z3ky92mwR_SWYezF7OMZnSLcDB7WuRMKp3Q%3D%3D)
He always dreamed of following in his dad’s footsteps to be a police officer. I also included one of my favorites of us from the night he graduated from the Police Academy.

I was so proud that night.

Never in my wildest imagination could I have predicted that my son would be _wrongfully_ _convicted_ _of_ _murder_ **– murder of a criminal my son didn’t even touch.**

[**_I’m praying you will help me save my son_**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=19870903dcac47978f096cb621b219fc&_e=VfO086_jlF6Od5lAsO5tjdpDpA6ZKg0GhnM1qVP-NtElyut7eJjinnAsHj3_CI3NFsGXzFWbL-Ebyw9_VqSfSiG6sQJtnCD3oc-otN2DR4y5nf5e811A5IbglH9ERKQy9ZTkubcGpOFKGn4GQZefQEpJw-RVLU_s280heWhkxiXSp2k1w3MxSpAOPGrdZzJ4uSFB9jRhIMAEAriPuaRyYHreLqhCcwMIHG4O4XhSiTmJedJJDJI1fi2m1XR5B-Xb4xzIAMUakEd2yf8S_3KtMtQK_Z3Zi049d7dWvwq5mwg4K0ZITIUNsfWKyUfoZivAZA3lsUv3IFMruS6HhBdRsNibN5qdQvtGV89hLkitfhc8zjQ7bjBBazi5ecFCeAv8G3GFARVD9NZSmxinuo1bFA%3D%3D) – **but first, let me explain what happened.** Our nightmare started in October of 2020.

Terence was sitting in his patrol car when he received a report that a known gang member, Karon Hylton-Brown, was seen entering a rival gang’s territory.

Karon was a violent criminal with a rap sheet a mile long – including drugs, illegal gun possession, and assaults. Earlier that day, he was seen arguing with a rival gang member who owed him money. So Terence and other officers were concerned Karon was planning to get revenge.

When Terence saw Karon illegally driving a motorized scooter on the sidewalk without a helmet, he tried to pull him over to talk. Instead, Karon took off on the scooter.

Terence watched as Karon sped through traffic, weaving in and out of cars and blowing past red lights and stop signs.

My son knew Karon was putting others at risk, so for 3 minutes he slowly and carefully followed behind in his patrol car.

Then it happened.

Karon veered into an alleyway, drove out the other side without looking and slammed into a passing car.

After several days in the hospital, Karon died from his injuries. And here’s where the story takes a shocking turn...

Because Karon was black, and the incident happened just four months after George Floyd’s death, the anti-police mob went into frenzy.

They blamed Terence for the accident and smeared him as a racist.

There were protests and even riots. The mob threw rocks at officers, smashed store windows, and set fires.

Anti-police politicians piled on too. Without any evidence, they demanded my son be charged even though he did nothing to cause Karon’s death.

**The pressure campaign worked and prosecutors charged my son with Second-Degree Murder.**

**Terence was convicted in December 2022 and he was finally just sentenced – to 5 1⁄2 years in prison.**

Now you understand why I’m so heartbroken. The injustice is almost too much to bear.

My son was well-respected by the community he served. He trained many Rookies and set a good example for all of them. He was even awarded Officer of the Year!

But unless Terence’s appeal is successful, he’s going to prison – which is why I’m urgently turning to you for help today. The **Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund** (LELDF) has stepped forward to pay for Terence’s defense and is actively raising funds to pay for the appeal.

If you’ve ever heard of LELDF, you know they’re a national group fighting on behalf of wrongly-accused police officers like Terence for over 30 years. LELDF has helped dozens of innocent cops – and their families – at their lowest moments.

We expect it will cost at least $150,000 to appeal Terence’s conviction.

So I have to ask you one of the toughest questions I’ve ever asked anyone:

[**_Will you help my son Terence fight the unjust charges against him by donating to LELDF today?_**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=19870903dcac47978f096cb621b219fc&_e=VfO086_jlF6Od5lAsO5tjdpDpA6ZKg0GhnM1qVP-NtElyut7eJjinnAsHj3_CI3NFsGXzFWbL-Ebyw9_VqSfSiG6sQJtnCD3oc-otN2DR4y5nf5e811A5IbglH9ERKQy9ZTkubcGpOFKGn4GQZefQEpJw-RVLU_s280heWhkxiXSp2k1w3MxSpAOPGrdZzJ4uSFB9jRhIMAEAriPuaRyYHreLqhCcwMIHG4O4XhSiTmc4Vti5UFR53roYTT8HIqbfl2OXkIWr-R_ZhshcyiQ1D_I8XH2G1_msaEXLSJP25hUB0vhaLryuy8ypHRsa95mgDADP_058OCYaFTCGE10gb5WEAFpTxhMcDyEWG_UlVT4CjflwVMYrp1XFmzL9TKjydIxX5KJdNzI3SS5m2UAmA%3D%3D)

I’m not asking you to donate the entire $150,000 yourself – though it would be a huge weight off our shoulders if you are able to help that way.

[**_But your tax-deductible gift of $35, $50, $100, or more will help LELDF fully fund Terence’s appeal over the next 12 months._**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=19870903dcac47978f096cb621b219fc&_e=VfO086_jlF6Od5lAsO5tjdpDpA6ZKg0GhnM1qVP-NtElyut7eJjinnAsHj3_CI3NFsGXzFWbL-Ebyw9_VqSfSiG6sQJtnCD3oc-otN2DR4y5nf5e811A5IbglH9ERKQy9ZTkubcGpOFKGn4GQZefQEpJw-RVLU_s280heWhkxiXSp2k1w3MxSpAOPGrdZzJ4uSFB9jRhIMAEAriPuaRyYHreLqhCcwMIHG4O4XhSiTmHK6fJZaR2ijM2z5NolnfaT-OPvNqYNLu9XA1MEs7fdLDa5F7rmMwJXK8BOVRYrKSb0BMeOSYS_taQXLMFaqIPVo7ofV1gldPgIDKoF7lzYtTOVcFyijyGGfIsrBpi-FVjfPZKBBRdoI61poWIdaO-rd5xCUcJ9evbPu0QUWwLqw%3D%3D) If I had anywhere else to turn, I wouldn’t be so bold as to ask for your help.

But I already lost my husband. He was a police officer too, and he was so proud that Terence followed in his footsteps. I simply can’t bear the thought of having another family member ripped away from me.

[**_So if you are at all able, please follow this link to make a tax-deductible donation to the Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund to help my Terence._**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=19870903dcac47978f096cb621b219fc&_e=VfO086_jlF6Od5lAsO5tjdpDpA6ZKg0GhnM1qVP-NtElyut7eJjinnAsHj3_CI3NFsGXzFWbL-Ebyw9_VqSfSiG6sQJtnCD3oc-otN2DR4y5nf5e811A5IbglH9ERKQy9ZTkubcGpOFKGn4GQZefQEpJw-RVLU_s280heWhkxiXSp2k1w3MxSpAOPGrdZzJ4uSFB9jRhIMAEAriPuaRyYHreLqhCcwMIHG4O4XhSiTmy_QhpTWDCE14omK9W9Vby9FIlonBW9vDDgEVXUa_2RM_pImjiuK6tX2-baT1khCzSPParbH3dK1OrLLqpBVMQttEADq7EbVLetg2aosnrPcFA55Sj-BhIwYIuM5yOhEVHly3E0rZ_e60kyw6NSI8DrrxkGaDN-K3jm4dHi4ft-w%3D%3D)

**Any amount – $35, $50, $100, or even $500 or more – will help.**

And because LELDF is a non-profit organization, your gift is completely tax-deductible. But whatever you can afford to give, I ask that you rush it back quickly.

Right now, Terence can only rely on the good nature and generosity of kind folks like you.

Thank you for taking the time to read my letter today.

And thank you in advance for helping LELDF support Terence and other wrongly accused officers.

Gratefully,
**Patti Reilly**

P.S. Don’t get me wrong, I’m thankful the judge granted Terence bond so he doesn’t have to report to prison just yet. Hopefully it never comes to that... but to get Terence’s conviction overturned, it’s going to cost at least $150,000 to fund his appeal. [**_Will you please help me save my son by making an urgent tax-deductible donation of $35, $50, $100 or more to LELDF today?_**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=19870903dcac47978f096cb621b219fc&_e=VfO086_jlF6Od5lAsO5tjdpDpA6ZKg0GhnM1qVP-NtElyut7eJjinnAsHj3_CI3NFsGXzFWbL-Ebyw9_VqSfSiG6sQJtnCD3oc-otN2DR4y5nf5e811A5IbglH9ERKQy9ZTkubcGpOFKGn4GQZefQEpJw-RVLU_s280heWhkxiXSp2k1w3MxSpAOPGrdZzJ4uSFB9jRhIMAEAriPuaRyYHreLqhCcwMIHG4O4XhSiTko8PAAh26d_T1PjWJyOhtvir_Vn-mGIT3ApdaVMzRIfqFjGmosYa-Wv6z2lMN1UBQR6H_pR1-iIJTIBknLjvtGEGJwjZ7u8z6fo36pYQXgOcGHOs6uLQ4WRmIDRSAZqptMB_dDU9h8yQgsyGt8TPrK-SnzXsZHwGPkShBM-XvM3w%3D%3D) Thank you for standing with Terence during this nightmare.
--------

_More such officers who sacrifice so much for will face similar fates unless we speak out and stand up for the brave men and women in blue. The next time you see a police officer in your community, please thank them for their service. To read more about our mission or the other Law Enforcement officers we're defending, please visit**www.PoliceDefense.org**. And remember -Blue Lives Matter!_

clean_body: Never in my wildest imagination could I have predicted that my son would be wrongfully convicted of murder – murder of a criminal my son didn’t even touch. Dear Peter, My son, Washington Metro Police Officer Terence Sutton, was just sentenced to 5 1⁄2 years in prison for doing the job he was trained to do. And though you and I have never met, I’m praying you’ll help me save him. Here are some photos of Terence as a little boy. He always dreamed of following in his dad’s footsteps to be a police officer. I also included one of my favorites of us from the night he graduated from the Police Academy. I was so proud that night. Never in my wildest imagination could I have predicted that my son would be wrongfully convicted of murder – murder of a criminal my son didn’t even touch. – but first, let me explain what happened. Our nightmare started in October of 2020. Terence was sitting in his patrol car when he received a report that a known gang member, Karon Hylton-Brown, was seen entering a rival gang’s territory. Karon was a violent criminal with a rap sheet a mile long – including drugs, illegal gun possession, and assaults. Earlier that day, he was seen arguing with a rival gang member who owed him money. So Terence and other officers were concerned Karon was planning to get revenge. When Terence saw Karon illegally driving a motorized scooter on the sidewalk without a helmet, he tried to pull him over to talk. Instead, Karon took off on the scooter. Terence watched as Karon sped through traffic, weaving in and out of cars and blowing past red lights and stop signs. My son knew Karon was putting others at risk, so for 3 minutes he slowly and carefully followed behind in his patrol car. Then it happened. Karon veered into an alleyway, drove out the other side without looking and slammed into a passing car. After several days in the hospital, Karon died from his injuries. And here’s where the story takes a shocking turn... Because Karon was black, and the incident happened just four months after George Floyd’s death, the anti-police mob went into frenzy. They blamed Terence for the accident and smeared him as a racist. There were protests and even riots. The mob threw rocks at officers, smashed store windows, and set fires. Anti-police politicians piled on too. Without any evidence, they demanded my son be charged even though he did nothing to cause Karon’s death. The pressure campaign worked and prosecutors charged my son with Second-Degree Murder. Terence was convicted in December 2022 and he was finally just sentenced – to 5 1⁄2 years in prison. Now you understand why I’m so heartbroken. The injustice is almost too much to bear. My son was well-respected by the community he served. He trained many Rookies and set a good example for all of them. He was even awarded Officer of the Year! But unless Terence’s appeal is successful, he’s going to prison – which is why I’m urgently turning to you for help today. The Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund (LELDF) has stepped forward to pay for Terence’s defense and is actively raising funds to pay for the appeal. If you’ve ever heard of LELDF, you know they’re a national group fighting on behalf of wrongly-accused police officers like Terence for over 30 years. LELDF has helped dozens of innocent cops – and their families – at their lowest moments. We expect it will cost at least $150,000 to appeal Terence’s conviction. So I have to ask you one of the toughest questions I’ve ever asked anyone: I’m not asking you to donate the entire $150,000 yourself – though it would be a huge weight off our shoulders if you are able to help that way. If I had anywhere else to turn, I wouldn’t be so bold as to ask for your help. But I already lost my husband. He was a police officer too, and he was so proud that Terence followed in his footsteps. I simply can’t bear the thought of having another family member ripped away from me. Any amount – $35, $50, $100, or even $500 or more – will help. And because LELDF is a non-profit organization, your gift is completely tax-deductible. But whatever you can afford to give, I ask that you rush it back quickly. Right now, Terence can only rely on the good nature and generosity of kind folks like you. Thank you for taking the time to read my letter today. And thank you in advance for helping LELDF support Terence and other wrongly accused officers. Gratefully, Patti Reilly P.S. Don’t get me wrong, I’m thankful the judge granted Terence bond so he doesn’t have to report to prison just yet. Hopefully it never comes to that... but to get Terence’s conviction overturned, it’s going to cost at least $150,000 to fund his appeal. Thank you for standing with Terence during this nightmare. More such officers who sacrifice so much for will face similar fates unless we speak out and stand up for the brave men and women in blue. The next time you see a police officer in your community, please thank them for their service. To read more about our mission or the other Law Enforcement officers we're defending, please visitwww.PoliceDefense.org. And remember -Blue Lives Matter! (C) Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund | 2560 Huntington Ave. Suite 203, Alexandria, VA, 22303 Click here to

Right now, America's biggest adversaries are working to surpass our nation's military capabilities. And critical problems exist in many areas of the world:
* Countries like Iran act deceitfully and use their military to bully other countries.

* Rogue states like North Korea and Syria pose direct threats to their regions and America's security.

* And the leaders of countries like China and Russia have proven they will do whatever they can to increase government control and state surveillance of their citizens.

On top of all this, our country's military equipment--including planes, tanks, and ships--is decades-old and years past its useful service.

With information like this, you would think our elected leaders would push to ensure a strong future for our nation's military. But some of them are actually looking to reduce America's defense spending. Apparently, they would rather prioritize issues like climate change.

That's why I'm emailing you today.

I'm told that you are a conservative who wants to ensure that our country remains a beacon of hope, opportunity, and liberty for all the world. And I understand that you are just as concerned as I am about the future of America's defense.

[So, today, I want to make sure you get a FREE copy of The Heritage Foundation's _2024 Index of U.S. Military Strength_.](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/u/click?_t=5b1853ae48754bee8f471363fe4933a9&_m=3fde8663eb664ffa9c52190d926358c0&_e=C77FMEpHq5F5pFd-OleWvseeY9v1hLD561fMC2H6YTnm_oQEJ8U-SCBReuYhluHS6KJBTj5AjThfhOofdvL8cuyadexnW_65bIvaMXwQ6DFenrP97F0aSWlgEniWrzkb29kxsUuFYVxD09ksau4FKdxe0MOW8M3SwW8tWtNvaziUUDxq9WMmhyBNSisdzneiqq3E8P40VRS-0zqyU6dkHa-pJKl7-x85nuNBcod5gltpgRmO-0-nkeAxdF1818DJO1VNbyhX8z2VhHEKUvDhBgqUIW7xa9VL-2kIYviA8lW1huo12xaOGK_qx4731PxpZ4Qb2MZLFllxhPs3ZjNquhzfjowkPNVGDeAqwryy6UqiVYnIX9x6I16bPfzCaKFS)

We publish this report to help decision-makers in government see how conditions have evolved in the past year and where American strengths, challenges, and opportunities for improvement lie.

But we also make this important resource available to the general public, so that you are aware of how well--or poorly--the government is handling this sacred task.

In addition to all of this, you can use this Index to explore topics like:
* Understanding risk in the great competition with the Chinese Communist Party.

* What we risk if we fail to fully modernize the U.S. nuclear deterrent.

* How prioritizing climate change and DEI training weakens America's military.

**You can get instant access to your FREE guide right now using this secure link:** [https://secured.heritage.org/us-military-strength-ebook/](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/u/click?_t=5b1853ae48754bee8f471363fe4933a9&_m=3fde8663eb664ffa9c52190d926358c0&_e=C77FMEpHq5F5pFd-OleWvseeY9v1hLD561fMC2H6YTnm_oQEJ8U-SCBReuYhluHS6KJBTj5AjThfhOofdvL8cuyadexnW_65bIvaMXwQ6DFenrP97F0aSWlgEniWrzkb29kxsUuFYVxD09ksau4FKdxe0MOW8M3SwW8tWtNvaziUUDxq9WMmhyBNSisdzneiqq3E8P40VRS-0zqyU6dkHV333wOo6JoOX3e_jip2G6YmMSlHidmhqbVyDKtJLva17mtvAz1wOkOr2Mfap5DZLtXkXF1xI2BMi9aA1eRQ16Uck064Lq-GlgGDUnDy1MCOiJQW2uvlwrF5dJITIKF72cnC8tQwmkTUKlhSTq84R6th26OsF-gtSa0aDXQwX9H6).

There's some eye-opening information in here, so I really hope you'll get your copy today. And you can share it with anyone you think would benefit from it.

**[Use this link to get your _2024 Index of U.S Military Strength_ today! >>](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/u/click?_t=5b1853ae48754bee8f471363fe4933a9&_m=3fde8663eb664ffa9c52190d926358c0&_e=C77FMEpHq5F5pFd-OleWvseeY9v1hLD561fMC2H6YTnm_oQEJ8U-SCBReuYhluHS6KJBTj5AjThfhOofdvL8cuyadexnW_65bIvaMXwQ6DFenrP97F0aSWlgEniWrzkb29kxsUuFYVxD09ksau4FKdxe0MOW8M3SwW8tWtNvaziUUDxq9WMmhyBNSisdzneiqq3E8P40VRS-0zqyU6dkHe5Qymz-Bwsd2jURmpENFSO2MZK7bLcGSpZlHjxdWRnY1lfp7Y1SziyY8tF3do2nXtcUk8Djm4BhglXmVA_dl6ZvHfVO0c8bptjyaC8SDAtMNi_dcwsVqJHNzBeEdatvbai0VwSiHQjMtDw6lhfWnasYKjMnSATjbDKEBm9WbE50)**

Best,

Tony Johnson
Director of Membership

name: Clint St. Mosley
subject: Clint St. Mosley: My Sincere Thanks
body:
Clint St Mosley: My Sincere Thanks | | | | |
---
| | | | | **My Sincere Thanks to All of You!**
---
| | | | | Hello everyone –
Election day was a very difficult day for my family, our team and myself. I can imagine many of you felt much the same. While it was personally disappointing to see our Congressional campaign come up short, the harder part for Susan and I was watching another opportunity to see a woman ascend to the White House denied.
The hopes so many of us had for a Kamala Harris administration were genuine. Seeing her positive energy and joyful campaign gave us hope.
I see a lot or parallels here. We too had a short amount of time to stand up our campaign and get things up and running. We also had a lot of work to do to: drafting policy priorities, networking with local clubs and campaigns, and setting up fundraising operations.
It’s not my place here to be the pundit and make claims of how or why things went wrong for Democrats this year, but I can say this: From a Congressional point of view, lower turnout at the top of the ballot meant less votes for the down-ballot candidates. In most cases, we saw an average 5% drop for Democrats in Congressional and Senate races across the country. We may end up seeing Kamala get 10M less votes than Joe Biden did in 2020.
Still, there’s a lot of good news out there. Our good friends Elissa Slotkin and Haley Stevens, managed to maneuver through the headwinds to advance to the U.S. Senate and maintain the 10th Congressional seat. Overall, Republican Congressional gains were slim. And progressive policy initiatives continued to win out in many states including minimum wage, sick leave and strengthening reproductive freedom.
**My Many, Many Thanks** As with every campaign, there are hundreds, if not thousands of volunteers, donors, advisors and supporters among the Democratic clubs, state campaign organizations and more. I cannot thank you all enough for all the time, love and financial support you provided our campaign.
My team: Todd Davis, Brian Mason, Austin Clark, Lori St.Germain, Tom Cruz, Jay Glauser, Sara DiGiglio, James Baldiga, and all of the volunteers, party chairs and bad asses that made things run and work behind the scenes: I thank you.
This goes too, for all the candidates, labor unions, and political organizations for your support and endorsements.
I cringe to write these because I always forget a name or two along the way – my apologies if I missed anyone but do know your help was essential and very much appreciated.
And my thanks to the 1,517+ donors from across the 9th CD – and literally across the United States! I am humbled to think that people as far away as Hawaii cared enough about our democracy to donate to our campaign. I of course, appreciate each and every one of the local donors who gave more than financial support – it was moral support for those days along the way when things got rough.
**Let’s Keep Up the Good Fight** Friends, if I’ve learned anything throughout this process is, no matter what happens at the polls, there’s always gains to be had, even in loss. I have met some of the most amazing, good natured, honest people ever – people who care about others, who take their time to give back to their communities and sacrifice to bring a better, more just and equitable America.
1\. Let’s stay connected. Please follow my Facebook page [@Clint4Congress](https://clint4congress.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e73040a2243d332c2516e1117&id=fc43d6d4d6&e=21c900a6be) to stay in touch with other CD-9 supporters and for future events, get-togethers and the like. The name might change but the community will remain the same.
2\. “Action absorbs anxiety. Mourn and move on.” Don’t give up, do something! I know we’re all feeling a lot of pain and angst, but time will heal the hurt, and we’ll regain our footings and get back to the work that we’re all so passionate about.
Join your local Democratic club, if you haven’t already. These are the critical hubs where like-minded folks work together to do things in their communities, like backpack giveaways, senior citizen volunteers, and tax preparation volunteers – along with the traditional organizational activities. We need more volunteers in these groups, especially Millennial and Gen-Z members to keep our party vibrant and growing.
Become a Precinct Delegate – or start a group in your area. These are elected members of the Democratic Party who help keep their neighbors and community involved throughout the year, and helps get out the vote during elections.
And finally, there are a number of groups online and in-person who are engaged in strengthening our democracy and building important relationships among progressive and like-minded people. Among them [Emily’s List](https://clint4congress.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e73040a2243d332c2516e1117&id=6afee40c5d&e=21c900a6be), [Red, Wine and Blue](https://clint4congress.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e73040a2243d332c2516e1117&id=5bcd9e601c&e=21c900a6be), [Fems for Democracy](https://clint4congress.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e73040a2243d332c2516e1117&id=82abb57d6d&e=21c900a6be) and [Distill Social](https://clint4congress.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e73040a2243d332c2516e1117&id=aca9a852eb&e=21c900a6be).
Again, my thanks to each and every one of you. Let’s keep hope alive and let’s all work to make sure our democracy remains healthy and strong – and that we leave our kids a world that’s kinder, more equitable, just and sustainable for the generations to come.
In solidarity,
Clint St. Mosley
---
| | | | [View email in browser](https://mailchi.mp/clint4congress.com/clint-st-mosley-vs-lisa-mcclain-the-final-stretch-5676957?e=21c900a6be)
Clint St. Mosley for Congress * 1301 Addington Ct * Lake Orion, MI 48360-2529 * USA
[update your preferences](https://clint4congress.us17.list-manage.com/profile?u=e73040a2243d332c2516e1117&id=a24a73b903&e=21c900a6be&c=9935488df0) or [unsubscribe](https://clint4congress.us17.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=e73040a2243d332c2516e1117&id=a24a73b903&t=b&e=21c900a6be&c=9935488df0)
---

I of course, appreciate each and every one of the local donors who gave more than financial support – it was moral support for those days along the way when things got rough. Let’s Keep Up the Good Fight Friends, if I’ve learned anything throughout this process is, no matter what happens at the polls, there’s always gains to be had, even in loss. I have met some of the most amazing, good natured, honest people ever – people who care about others, who take their time to give back to their communities and sacrifice to bring a better, more just and equitable America. 1\. Let’s stay connected. Please follow my Facebook page to stay in touch with other CD-9 supporters and for future events, get-togethers and the like. The name might change but the community will remain the same. 2\. “Action absorbs anxiety. Mourn and move on.” Don’t give up, do something! I know we’re all feeling a lot of pain and angst, but time will heal the hurt, and we’ll regain our footings and get back to the work that we’re all so passionate about. Join your local Democratic club, if you haven’t already. These are the critical hubs where like-minded folks work together to do things in their communities, like backpack giveaways, senior citizen volunteers, and tax preparation volunteers – along with the traditional organizational activities. We need more volunteers in these groups, especially Millennial and Gen-Z members to keep our party vibrant and growing. Become a Precinct Delegate – or start a group in your area. These are elected members of the Democratic Party who help keep their neighbors and community involved throughout the year, and helps get out the vote during elections. And finally, there are a number of groups online and in-person who are engaged in strengthening our democracy and building important relationships among progressive and like-minded people. Among them , , and . Again, my thanks to each and every one of you. Let’s keep hope alive and let’s all work to make sure our democracy remains healthy and strong – and that we leave our kids a world that’s kinder, more equitable, just and sustainable for the generations to come. In solidarity, Clint St. Mosley | | | | Clint St. Mosley for Congress 1301 Addington Ct Lake Orion, MI 48360-2529 * USA or |

We've fought too hard to give up now just inches away from the finish line. | | "Generic Button" Friend, we're counting on support from grassroots patriots just like you. The Democrats will think they have this race locked up and have an army of lawyers to drag this out. I'm counting on you, Friend. Can you rush an emergency donation today? - Michelle Steel | | Paid for by Michelle Steel for Congress PO Box 472 Surfside, CA 90743 | To ensure that you don't miss out on important messages, please add "info@email.michellesteelca.com" to your address book. Not Sure How? Follow the link below to your email provider to get step by step instructions on how to add us to your address book. , , , ,

# [Thank you for everything](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=151726346&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUxNzI2MzQ2LCJpYXQiOjE3MzE3MjA4NTcsImV4cCI6MTczNDMxMjg1NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.WnUiJyAXFI_rAluxjHGfUIkWcFOw42om8qRecVCy7sA)
| | [Lucas Kunce](https://substack.com/@lucaskunce)
---
| Nov 16
---
| [](https://substack.com/@lucaskunce)
---
---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=151726346&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUxNzI2MzQ2LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTczMTcyMDg1NywiZXhwIjoxNzM0MzEyODU3LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTg2Nzc0MSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.U-zKqZQfk2ofZfafzDWgAGjNwdZ7loPeKZcS1gIq6vE&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=151726346&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUxNzI2MzQ2LCJpYXQiOjE3MzE3MjA4NTcsImV4cCI6MTczNDMxMjg1NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.WnUiJyAXFI_rAluxjHGfUIkWcFOw42om8qRecVCy7sA&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=151726346&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUxNzI2MzQ2LCJpYXQiOjE3MzE3MjA4NTcsImV4cCI6MTczNDMxMjg1NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.WnUiJyAXFI_rAluxjHGfUIkWcFOw42om8qRecVCy7sA)
---
| | [](https://substack..5ZF4KiG7wfxQlnpG3slZt539Ye88AnYnXtjPKmwUwos?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/lucaskunce/p/thank-you-for-everything?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---
It's been 10 days since the election and I've had a lot to think about.
The thing that I've thought about the most is how incredible and supportive everyone was out on the campaign trail, both physical and virtual.
I can't count the number of people who gave us friendship bracelets and cookies, welcomed us into their homes, donated, and thanked us for the work we did in this race.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/24a69aa1-116f-4dd9-80c8-234ba6e07d91?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Marilyn showing off some of our bracelets_
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/765684e4-8ad7-4803-8d6b-9875ad78be1d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Cookies!_
It's true that it's very hard for a normal person to run for office in this country, especially if, like we did, you choose not to sell out. I had to quit my job, live off of savings, and on the road for a couple of years, which was a real challenge. But it never felt like a sacrifice, because so many of you were behind us and because we stood for something real.
I'll send out a more thorough review of the campaign, and what we accomplished together, soon. But here are some toplines.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b8362015-5554-4fa4-86fe-24cc628914e1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Independence, MO_
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b126ab5c-cd02-4d5b-9518-5c545b10ccda?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Festus, MO_
**Background**
* We built a historic grassroots movement, with support from nearly 250,000 individual donors and Missourians in all 114 counties and the City of St. Louis
* We received zero dollars from the national party, corporate PACs, federal lobbyists, or Big Pharma and Big Oil executives
* Unlike recent Democratic candidates for Senate in Missouri who received tens of millions of dollars in external support, we received almost none
* We were outspent on TV thanks to the millions of dollars raised for Josh Hawley by DC insiders like Senator John Cornyn, as well as millions of dollars in attack ads from a billionaire-funded Super PAC supporting Hawley
* Being outspent and not having the same backup that Hawley got meant we had to target our spending to just four of Missouri's ten media markets, largely on TV and digital -- the need to both get our message out and defend ourselves on air in those four media markets made it impossible to afford things like a proper mail program or rural radio. Our goal was to produce a strong number of ticket-splitting voters in the exurban counties around Kansas City and St. Louis, as well as parts of central and southern Missouri

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d1f49626-6b7b-486b-ae56-7b2b86e583ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_This was one of our longest running ads. It highlighted my family struggling through bankruptcy, how our community took care of us, and how now communities like where I grew up have been stripped for parts and left behind. It also highlighted my commitment to rebuilding our state and restoring power and freedom to everyday people._
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/51db9121-44ba-4adf-817e-b30de4da9e08?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_This was our closing ad of the campaign. It highlighted how my opponent was getting rich while the rest of us struggled, and my commitment to investing in Missourians._
**Results**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7f9527a8-378f-4614-ba5e-0dcbc4c22a93?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* We outperformed the margin in the presidential race and other statewide races by roughly 5% to 10% across the board -- a better performance than many Democratic candidates for Senate around the country this cycle. We even achieved a similar margin to several states that received significantly more attention and financial support
* We received roughly the same number of votes that previous competitive Democratic statewide candidates in Missouri have gotten in the last 10-15 years -- but also saw a return of the boosted GOP turnout in a presidential cycle that we first saw in 2020, with a couple hundred thousand more GOP voters compared to 2016
* In many targeted counties where we were able to spend to compete, like Jefferson, Ray, and Iron, we outperformed the presidential ticket's margin by roughly 10% or more
* We received more votes in St. Louis City than any other statewide candidate, and more votes in Kansas City than even the presidential ticket
* In places where we didn't have the resources to introduce me to voters, like in the Hannibal media market for example, the margins in our race were much closer to standard partisan breakdowns -- comparing those results to where we were able to advertise shows just how effective our message was when we were able to get it out
* We thankfully still saw citizen-led initiatives to end Missouri's total abortion ban, raise the minimum wage, and establish paid sick leave succeed on the statewide ballot

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/81c1ce08-0073-427c-811f-55494fa2efd6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Wentzville, MO_
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/0aa0e924-c4c5-43da-9f2b-57471be1e7a9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Springfield, MO_
We have a lot to be proud of. When we were able to introduce my background and ideas to people, we really over-performed. In a better year, with the resources to compete statewide, that could change.
No matter the final result, I will always know that if votes were weighted by enthusiasm, we would have won in a landslide. You showed up, consistently, to give us the best fighting chance possible. I am so grateful to everyone who dug deep, helped spread the word, and kept us in the game.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ef6eb47c-ec96-4b74-bfd5-a581d0e67809?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
More to follow.
Thank you for everything.
Lucas
Use of military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform do not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a94d1799-373d-4f61-8c80-a0bc75e9068f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

name: PA Senate Alert
subject: Here’s what you can do today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Debbie for Florida
body:
we absolutely cannot afford to lose another Senate seat
| | |
---
**Friend , this Pennsylvania Senate race is not over, and we need your help to re-elect Bob Casey.**
If we’re going to stop Republicans from attacking our fundamental freedoms, we can’t afford to lose another Senate seat.
Bob Casey has fought to protect women’s rights, workers’ rights, and voting rights his entire career, and he won’t stop now.
**The good news is that this race is not over, and votes are still being counted in Pennsylvania.** Our team is working hard to make that happen, but with tens of thousands of votes left to be counted, we need your help.
_[Split a donation of $5 or more between Bob Casey and Debbie Mucarsel-Powell today to ensure we have the resources we need to make sure Pennsylvanians’ voices are heard.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97165984/512529608/1089970663?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk2538-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS8xMDc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDNkM2I5ZmItYmFhMy1lZjExLTg4ZDAtNjA0NWJkZDYyZGI2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=XlHR4nfIFfBkl6RmV2yENvphX0DZ_Xgg32b4EP-jYQM=)_

name: Tom Fitton
subject: West Point DEI Update!
body:
USAID Hiding Where $7M Gaza Grant Went

[INSIDE JW]

Judicial Watch Sues for Records on Rebranding of West Point’s DEI
Office

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71121&L=33892&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vbGl2ZS9pYlhjRzlUMVNJYz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=a1999dd25f8edc7683423c7e290e47652a01bb45a6153f579e333a68525ff4e8]
It seems games are afoot at West Point to disguise its radical
diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) agenda.

We filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71121&L=33873&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9kLXdlc3QtcG9pbnQtZGVpLWNvbXBsYWludC0wMjk0MS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=a12d39c3de81130a526b5dfa2583c6aef93e988b639f7d13a6baa53147ec2257]
against the U.S. Department of Defense for information regarding the
rebranding of West Point’s DEI office to “Office of Engagement and
Retention” (_Judicial Watch Inc. v. U.S. Department of Defense_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71121&L=33873&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9kLXdlc3QtcG9pbnQtZGVpLWNvbXBsYWludC0wMjk0MS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=a12d39c3de81130a526b5dfa2583c6aef93e988b639f7d13a6baa53147ec2257]
(No. 1:24-cv-02941)).

We sued in the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia after
the United States Military Academy at West Point failed to respond to
an August 28, 2024, FOIA request for:

* All documents related to the renaming of and/or elimination of
West Point’s “Office of Diversity, Inclusion and Equal
Opportunity” (ODIEO).

* All documents related to the creation of West Point’s “Office
of Engagement and Retention.”

* All emails related to the matters addressed in Bullets 1 and 2
sent to and from the following USMA officials: Superintendent LTG
Steve Gilland, Dean BG Shane Reeves, Commandant BG R.J. Garcia, and
Chief Diversity Officer Lisa Benitez.

Reporting in August 2024 detailed
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71121&L=33874&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9hcm1lZGZvcmNlcy5wcmVzcy93ZXN0LXBvaW50cy1kZWktb2ZmaWNlLWNoYW5nZXMtbmFtZS10by1vZmZpY2Utb2YtZW5nYWdlbWVudC1hbmQtcmV0ZW50aW9uLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=07e45e17d8d83fc5bd9464624a6e8f179c9bea445c014e12bbf60eef403460ff]
the name change:

> The West Point Office of “Diversity, Inclusion and Equal
> Opportunity” is now called the “Office of Engagement and
> Retention”.... On the West Point page
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71121&L=33875&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cud2VzdHBvaW50LmVkdS9hYm91dC9vZmZpY2Utb2YtZW5nYWdlbWVudC1hbmQtcmV0ZW50aW9uP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=1c568a455eaf1cd1d5f21dbc245d91162dd6c5ab2e2b9f2b85659634b86f93d7],
> there’s not much change beyond the title ...
We recently sued
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71121&L=33640&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvYWlyLWZvcmNlLWFjYWRlbXlzLWRlaS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=2b0618d89dabbdedf04feaea9c078f221f1b49afedac2e6d04e076701c687a89]
on behalf of
Stand Together Against Racism and Radicalism in the Services, Inc.
(STARRS) against the U.S. Department of Defense for records regarding
the U.S. Air Force Academy’s diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI)
plans.

In June 2024, we sued
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71121&L=32830&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvd2VzdC1wb2ludC1taXNzaW9uLXN0YXRlbWVudC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=004873e5747d44b61bec7204a555a7b30c8fed67203cbc3e99b828851c238135]
the
Defense Department for records regarding the deletion of the words
“Duty, Honor, Country” from the United States Military Academy at
West Point’s mission statement.

In March 2023, records
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71121&L=29154&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvYWlyLWZvcmNlLWFjYWRlbXktZm9jdXMtb24tY3J0Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=4caf6e703f5d606ec1200b50bc0794f7d9a84bd3c75277a5683d76b8d2763cfe]
we
obtained from the Department of Defense showed the Air Force Academy
has made race and gender instruction a top priority in the training of
cadets.

In July 2023, we exposed
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71121&L=29831&F=T&&hash=a1d5d4d84b34bce02a40178ecf93402b0c36948055236ddfe9d403c671271f61]
records from the United States Air Force Academy that included
instructional materials and emails that address topics such as
Critical Race Theory, “white privilege,” and Black Lives Matter.

In July 2022, we sued
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71121&L=27741&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanctc3Vlcy1mb3ItbmF2YWwtYWNhZGVteS1jcnQtcmVjb3Jkcy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=1d9f055dac8c9b21d59ad07af1356b2dbbee49775af2f0fd0c1e983ba4bb65f0]
the Department of Defense for records related to the United States
Naval Academy (USNA) implementing Critical Race Theory (CRT) in the
training of naval recruits.

In June 2022, we received records revealing Critical Race Theory
instruction at the U.S. Military Academy at West Point
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71121&L=27536&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY3J0LXByb3BhZ2FuZGEtYXQtd2VzdC1wb2ludC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=61298f14d739336b97230b419ac55e576f2e2a6a98ce23b8bdca56be0aeb2f62]
One
training slide contains a graphic titled “MODERN-DAY SLAVERY IN THE
USA.” [Emphasis in original].

JUDICIAL WATCH SUES OVER $27M GRANTS FOR USE IN GAZA

There’s something fishy in aid to the Middle East.

We filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71121&L=33877&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtdXNhaWQtZ2F6YS1jb21wbGFpbnQtMDIxNTkvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=edf7114ffb5b249d06d6b045e0c689a951fcf97af932a3736b017eb2eca6ff12]
against the United States Agency for International Development (USAID)
for records about $27 million in U.S. grants awarded to
“Miscellaneous Foreign Awardees” that have been designated for use
in Gaza (_Judicial Watch v. U.S. Agency for International Development_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71121&L=33877&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtdXNhaWQtZ2F6YS1jb21wbGFpbnQtMDIxNTkvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=edf7114ffb5b249d06d6b045e0c689a951fcf97af932a3736b017eb2eca6ff12]
(No. 1:24-cv-02159)).

On April 2, 2024, we filed a FOIA request with the USAID for:

> 1. All records identifying the recipients of USAID funding under the
> $7,000,000 grant allocation awarded on or about November 15, 2023,
> and associated with Federal Award Identification Number
> 720BHA24GR00005.
>
> 2. All proposals, applications, scope of work documents, or similar
> records related to any grant award or sub-award associated with
> Federal Award Identification Number 720BHA24GR00005.
Recently USAID produced records
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71121&L=33878&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtdXNhaWQtZ2F6YS1yZWNvcmRzLTAyMTU5Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=dea9632e6857466d711aa3e4dc8aacab29312dd12ef10d168454f74d0b950da3]
in this case but is refusing to disclose what organizations received
the money. We are challenging that withholding. USAID reports
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71121&L=33882&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9mb3JlaWduYXNzaXN0YW5jZS5nb3YvY2Qvd2VzdC1iYW5rLWdhemEvMjAyMy9vYmxpZ2F0aW9ucy8xP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=913e0719a5f50b182f339af0ba7cb8e24f26a010bd24529be06aad3da1378dab]
that over $282 million was obligated to the West Bank and Gaza in
fiscal year 2023.

On October 7, 2023, Hamas—a U.S.-designated terrorist
organization—invaded southwest Israel
